prestazioni: Engine swap to a modernized 6.75L V8 (originally the Rolls-Royce L-series, but upgraded with a bespoke billet aluminum block by Cosworth Engineering, retaining the original 90° V8 architecture but reinforced with forged internals, including a steel crankshaft from a Bentley Mulsanne and Mahle Motorsport forged pistons with a 9.5:1 compression ratio for forced induction compatibility)

Custom twin-turbocharger setup using Garrett GTX5533R turbos (water-cooled, ball-bearing) with 1.25 A/R turbine housings, paired with a custom air-to-water intercooler system by Bell Intercoolers (3-core, 1200hp-rated) and a bespoke intake manifold fabricated by JE Motorsport with individual throttle bodies (ITBs) for each cylinder, controlled via a Motec M150 ECU with flex-fuel and ethanol compatibility

Exhaust system overhaul featuring a custom 3.5-inch stainless steel exhaust with equal-length headers designed by Jet-Hot Coatings (ceramic-coated for heat management), terminating in a pair of Borla Pro XS mufflers (straight-through design, 304 stainless steel) with electronically controlled bypass valves for adjustable sound levels, integrated with a custom X-pipe crossover for optimized scavenging
estetica: Full custom widebody conversion by Carrozzeria Touring Superleggera, extending the fenders by 3.5 inches per side to accommodate 20x9.5-inch front and 20x12-inch rear BBS LM wheels (gold-painted centers, polished lips) wrapped in Michelin Pilot Sport Cup 2 tires (275/35/20 front, 325/30/20 rear) with custom offset for aggressive stance

Bespoke handcrafted aluminum body panels (replacing original steel) by Radford Coachbuilders, including a one-off front splitter with integrated carbon fiber canards (by Vorsteiner), a custom rear diffuser with active aerodynamics (adjustable angle via hydraulic actuators), and a carbon fiber hood with functional NACA ducts for intercooler and brake cooling

Interior reimagined by Saddleback Leather Co. with full Alcantara headliner, custom Recaro Pole Position seats (reclining, with embroidered Rolls-Royce crests), a bespoke Momo Prototipo steering wheel (wood-rimmed, with quick-release hub), and a digital instrument cluster by Racepak (V-Net data logging, configurable displays) integrated into the original dashboard with a hidden roll cage (T45 steel, chrome-moly) by Safety Devices
